context: The full name was retconned multiple times in the comics, but the MCU settled on Strategic Homeland Intervention, Enforcement and Logistics Division. Agent Coulson admits they're 'working on' the name. The organization was founded after WWII by Peggy Carter, Howard Stark, and others to protect against extraordinary threats.

question: What is the name of Doctor Strange's home base?
choices: Kamar-Taj, the sorcerers' training compound in Nepal
The Mirror Dimension, a pocket reality for trapping threats
The New York Nexus, a mystical convergence point
context: The Sanctum Sanctorum is located at 177A Bleecker Street in Greenwich Village, New York, and is one of three Sanctums (along with London and Hong Kong) that protect Earth from mystical threats. Its Seal of Vishanti window is recognizable from the comics. The building exists in real life, though it's just an ordinary building.

question: Who is Peter Parker's best friend in Spider-Man: Homecoming?
choices: Flash Thompson, Peter's academic rival and bully
Harry Osborn, the son of Norman Osborn
Miles Morales, a fellow teenage Spider-Man from Brooklyn
context: Ned Leeds, played by Jacob Batalon, is Peter's enthusiastic 'guy in the chair' who discovers Peter's secret identity and immediately wants to help. His character combines elements of several Spider-Man comic characters. His excited reaction to learning Peter is Spider-Man is one of Homecoming's funniest moments.

question: What is the name of the organization secretly embedded inside S.H.I.E.L.D.?
choices: A.I.M. (Advanced Idea Mechanics), a rogue science division
The Ten Rings, an ancient terrorist network with global reach
context: HYDRA infiltrated S.H.I.E.L.D. from its very founding, when Operation Paperclip recruited Arnim Zola and other former Nazi scientists. Their motto 'Cut off one head, two more shall take its place' proved literally true within the organization. Captain America: The Winter Soldier's revelation that HYDRA had been controlling S.H.I.E.L.D. for decades was the MCU's biggest conspiracy twist.

question: What is Thanos's home planet?
choices: Xandar, the Nova Corps' heavily populated home world
Sanctuary, the massive warship Thanos commands across the cosmos
Knowhere, the mining colony built inside a Celestial's skull
context: Titan was once a thriving civilization that collapsed due to overpopulation and resource depletion, according to Thanos. This trauma drove his obsession with 'balancing' the universe by eliminating half of all life. When the Avengers visit Titan in Infinity War, it's a desolate wasteland, physically representing Thanos's greatest fear for the universe.

question: What is Thanos's goal with the completed Infinity Gauntlet?
answer: Erase half of all life in the universe
choices: Gain immortality and rule as an eternal cosmic emperor
Erase half of all life in the universe
Conquer every inhabited planet and install his own order
Reverse the entropy that causes stars and worlds to die
context: Thanos believes that randomly eliminating half of all life will save the universe from the resource collapse that destroyed his homeworld Titan. He sees himself not as a villain but as a necessary surgeon. The 'Snap' (or 'Blip') erasing half of all life is one of cinema's most shocking villain victories.

question: Who is sacrificed to obtain the Soul Stone in Avengers: Infinity War?
choices: Black Widow, who volunteered herself willingly
context: Thanos throws Gamora, his adopted daughter, off the cliff on Vormir because she's the person he loves most. Gamora believed Thanos was incapable of love, but the Soul Stone accepted the sacrifice. Zoe Saldana's horrified realization that Thanos genuinely loves her in his twisted way is devastating.

question: What material is Captain America's shield made from?
choices: Adamantium, the indestructible metal bonded to Wolverine
Titanium-steel alloy forged by Howard Stark's engineering team
context: Vibranium is a rare metal found almost exclusively in Wakanda, brought to Earth by a meteorite millions of years ago. It absorbs kinetic energy (vibrations), making Cap's shield virtually indestructible and capable of absorbing any impact. Howard Stark used all available vibranium to create the shield, explaining why it's one of a kind.

question: In Endgame, who besides Thor is able to lift Mjolnir?
choices: Iron Man, who attempted but failed at the last moment
Vision, who lifted it effortlessly during Age of Ultron
Hulk, whose strength briefly overcame the enchantment
context: Captain America lifting Mjolnir is one of the MCU's most crowd-pleasing moments, as audiences erupted in theaters worldwide. Thor's delighted 'I knew it!' suggests Cap was always worthy but held back in Age of Ultron when he budged the hammer slightly. The enchantment states only the 'worthy' can wield it. Also referred to as: Steve Rogers.

question: What is the name of the Guardians of the Galaxy's ship?
choices: The Benatar, their sleek purple cruiser from later films
The Dark Aster, flagship of Ronan the Accuser's fleet
The Quadrant, a large cargo hauler used by the team
context: The Milano is Peter Quill's M-class ship named after his childhood crush, actress Alyssa Milano. Its orange-and-blue color scheme and compact design make it one of the MCU's most recognizable ships. After the Milano was destroyed, the team used the Benatar (named after Pat Benatar) in later films.

question: Which Infinity Stone is contained in the Tesseract?
choices: Power Stone, the destructive purple gem found on Morag
Mind Stone, the yellow gem embedded in Vision's forehead
Reality Stone, also known as the Aether from Thor: The Dark World
context: The Space Stone allows instantaneous travel across the universe and was the first Infinity Stone introduced in the MCU (Captain America: The First Avenger). Red Skull used it to power HYDRA weapons, Howard Stark recovered it from the ocean, and Loki used it to open the Chitauri portal. Thanos eventually crushed the Tesseract cube to extract the stone.

question: What is the name of Ant-Man's mentor and the original Ant-Man?
choices: Howard Stark, Tony's father and S.H.I.E.L.D. founder
Bill Foster, the scientist who became Black Goliath
Janet Van Dyne, the original Wasp and Pym's wife
context: Hank Pym discovered the Pym Particles that allow size manipulation and operated as the original Ant-Man alongside his wife Janet Van Dyne (the Wasp) during the Cold War. He retired after Janet was lost in the Quantum Realm and passed the mantle to Scott Lang. Michael Douglas brought gravitas to the role of the brilliant but bitter Pym.

question: Who kills Loki in Avengers: Infinity War?
choices: Corvus Glaive, using his near-indestructible glaive weapon
Proxima Midnight, with her enchanted spear
Ebony Maw, using his powerful telekinetic abilities
context: Thanos snaps Loki's neck after Loki's failed assassination attempt with a concealed dagger. Thanos's cold declaration 'No resurrections this time' seemed definitive. However, the 2012 timeline Loki escapes with the Tesseract during the Avengers' time heist in Endgame, leading to the Loki TV series.

question: What is Nick Fury's most recognizable physical feature?
answer: Eye patch over his left eye
choices: A prosthetic hand from a past combat injury
Eye patch over his left eye
A prominent scar running across his right cheek
Close-cropped silver hair and matching goatee
context: Nick Fury's eye patch over his left eye is his most distinctive feature, and he tells different people different stories about how he lost the eye. Captain Marvel reveals the anticlimactic truth: a Flerken (an alien cat named Goose) scratched it. Samuel L. Jackson has said the eye patch is his favorite part of the costume.

question: What is the Power Stone originally found inside in the MCU?
choices: A Ravager strongbox recovered from deep space
The Cosmi-Rod, a Kree ceremonial weapon
A sealed Asgardian vault on the planet Xandar
context: The Power Stone was contained inside the Orb, an artifact hidden in the Temple of Morag and sought by Star-Lord at the beginning of Guardians of the Galaxy. Direct contact with the stone unleashes energy that destroys most beings instantly. The Guardians survived wielding it together only because of their combined strength and Quill's Celestial heritage.

question: What is the final boss of Minecraft?
choices: The Wither, a three-headed boss summoned with soul sand
The Elder Guardian, a powerful underwater dungeon boss
The Warden, a blind hostile mob from the Deep Dark biome
context: The Ender Dragon resides in The End dimension, reached by finding and activating an End Portal with Eyes of Ender. Defeating it triggers Minecraft's iconic poem-style credits sequence, written by Julian Gough. The dragon can actually be resummoned using End Crystals for additional fights.

question: What material is needed to make a Nether Portal in Minecraft?
choices: Blackstone, found only in the Nether
Crying Obsidian, used for respawn anchors
Basalt, formed near magma blocks underground
context: Obsidian is formed when water flows over a lava source block, and it takes nearly 10 seconds to mine even with a diamond pickaxe. You need at least 10 blocks arranged in a 4x5 frame (corners optional) to create a working Nether Portal. The portal can also be created without a diamond pickaxe by carefully placing lava and water to form obsidian in place.

question: What is the name of the hostile mob that explodes when near a player?
context: The Creeper is so iconic it appears on official Minecraft merchandise and the game's logo. Its distinctive 'ssss' sound before exploding has become one of gaming's most recognizable audio cues. Charged Creepers, created when lightning strikes nearby, produce an even bigger explosion.

question: How many bookshelves are needed to unlock level 30 enchantments?
context: Exactly 15 bookshelves placed one block away from the enchanting table with air in between unlocks the maximum level 30 enchantments. Each bookshelf requires 3 books and 6 planks to craft, so reaching max enchanting takes 45 books total. The bookshelves must be on the same level as or one block above the table.

question: What mob drops Ender Pearls in Minecraft?
choices: Endermites, tiny mobs that spawn from Ender Pearl throws
Ender Dragon, the final boss of The End
context: Endermen are tall, dark, teleporting mobs that drop Ender Pearls when killed. These pearls are essential for locating and activating End Portals (when combined with Blaze Powder to make Eyes of Ender). Thrown Ender Pearls also teleport the player to where they land, at the cost of some fall damage.

question: What three skulls are needed to summon the Wither?
choices: Three Zombie heads placed on soul sand
Three Skeleton skulls gathered from strays
Three Creeper heads triggered by lightning
context: You need three Wither Skeleton skulls placed on top of four blocks of soul sand in a T-shape to summon the Wither. Wither Skeletons only spawn in Nether Fortresses, and the skull drop rate is just 2.5% without the Looting enchantment. The Wither is actually harder than the Ender Dragon for most players.

question: What is the rare biome where Mooshrooms naturally spawn?
choices: Dark Forest, home of the Woodland Mansion
Warped Forest in the Nether dimension
context: Mushroom Fields (also called Mushroom Islands) are extremely rare island biomes where no hostile mobs naturally spawn, making them uniquely safe. Mooshrooms are special cows covered in mushrooms that can be 'milked' with a bowl to get mushroom stew. Shearing a Mooshroom turns it into a regular cow and drops mushrooms.

question: What update added bees to Minecraft Java Edition?
context: The Buzzy Bees update (1.15) released in December 2019, adding bees, beehives, bee nests, honey blocks, and honeycomb. Bees pollinate crops and flowers, actually speeding up crop growth when they return to their hive. The honey block introduced unique properties like reducing fall damage and slowing movement. Also referred to as: Buzzy Bees.

question: What does a Nether Star drop from in Minecraft?
choices: The Ender Dragon, guardian of The End
The Elder Guardian in ocean monuments
The Warden lurking in the Deep Dark
context: The Wither is a player-summoned boss that drops a single Nether Star upon defeat. This star is used to craft a Beacon, one of the most powerful utility blocks in the game that grants area buffs like Speed and Strength. The Wither is the only renewable source of Nether Stars.

question: What enchantment allows a pickaxe to yield more ore drops?
choices: Silk Touch, which preserves the original block
Looting, which increases mob item drops
context: Fortune increases the number of items dropped when mining ores, with Fortune III roughly doubling average yields from diamond ore. It does not work on ores that drop themselves (like iron or gold ore before 1.17). Fortune and Silk Touch are mutually exclusive enchantments.

question: What new mob in Minecraft 1.19 is drawn to sound in the Deep Dark?
choices: Sculk Shrieker, which emits warning shrieks
Skulk Crawler, a multi-legged cave predator
Phantom, which attacks players who avoid sleep
context: The Warden is a terrifyingly powerful blind mob that hunts entirely by sound and vibration, introduced in the Wild Update (1.19). It has 500 HP (more than both bosses) and deals enough damage to kill a fully armored player in just two hits. Mojang designed it as a mob you're meant to sneak past, not fight.

question: What item is used to ignite a Nether Portal?
choices: Blaze Rod used as a fire starter
context: Flint and Steel is crafted from one iron ingot and one flint (dropped from gravel), and it ignites the purple portal effect inside an obsidian frame. Fire charges also work as an alternative lighter. In a pinch, players have used lava and flammable blocks to ignite portals without any flint and steel at all.

question: What is the rarest ore found exclusively in the Nether?
choices: Diamond ore that generates near Nether fortresses
Nether Gold Ore, found scattered throughout Netherrack
Quartz ore, common in Nether caves and cliffs
context: Ancient Debris spawns deep in the Nether (mostly around Y-level 15) and is blast-resistant, making bed explosions a popular mining strategy. Smelting it produces Netherite Scrap, and four scraps plus four gold ingots make one Netherite Ingot. Netherite gear is the strongest in the game and uniquely floats in lava.

question: What is the currency used to trade with villagers in Minecraft?
choices: Gold Ingots, valued by Piglins but not villagers
Diamonds, the most precious overworld gem
Lapis Lazuli, used in enchanting and decoration
context: Emeralds are Minecraft's trading currency, and each villager profession offers different trades that unlock as you level them up. Emerald ore is actually rarer than diamond ore in the overworld, found only in mountain biomes. Most players get emeralds through trading rather than mining.

question: What hostile mob in Minecraft drops a music disc when killed by a skeleton?
choices: Zombie, a common overworld undead mob
Drowned, the underwater variant of zombies
Skeleton, which can also be killed by other skeletons
context: When a Skeleton's arrow is the killing blow on a Creeper, the Creeper drops a random music disc. This mechanic encourages creative combat setups where players lure Creepers into skeleton firing lines. There are multiple discs with different tracks, composed by C418 and later Lena Raine.

question: What biome generates the tall spires called 'Chorus Plants' in Minecraft?
choices: Badlands, known for its mesa plateaus and terracotta
Mushroom Fields, the rarest overworld biome
Deep Dark, home to the Warden and ancient cities
context: Chorus Plants grow exclusively on the outer End islands, accessible after defeating the Ender Dragon and finding an End Gateway. Their fruit can be eaten to randomly teleport the player short distances, and smelted into purpur blocks for building. The End's outer islands also contain End Cities with Elytra wings.

question: What effect does the Silk Touch enchantment have?
answer: Allows you to mine blocks and pick them up as-is
choices: Allows you to mine blocks and pick them up as-is
Increases mining speed for all block types significantly
Doubles ore drops when used on diamond-level tools
Prevents blocks from taking durability damage when mined
context: Silk Touch lets you mine blocks and collect them in their original form rather than their usual drop. This means you can pick up glass without breaking it, collect spawners, or grab ice blocks. It's mutually exclusive with Fortune, creating an interesting strategic choice for your pickaxe.

question: Who are the main protagonists of Resident Evil 2 (original and remake)?
choices: Leon Kennedy and Ada Wong working undercover
Claire Redfield and Chris Redfield as siblings
context: Leon S. Kennedy is a rookie cop on his first day and Claire Redfield is searching for her brother Chris. They arrive in Raccoon City during the zombie outbreak and are separated, each experiencing different parts of the same nightmare. The dual-protagonist structure was revolutionary for survival horror.

question: What is the name of the virus used to create zombies in the original Resident Evil?
choices: G-Virus, developed from failed T-Virus experiments
The Mold, a fungal mutagenic organism
context: The T-Virus (Tyrant Virus) was developed by the Umbrella Corporation as a biological weapon, but leaked into the Arklay Mountains and Raccoon City water supply. It reanimates dead tissue, creating zombies and various other mutations. The T-Virus was derived from the ancient Progenitor Virus found in African flowers.

question: Who is the main antagonist Umbrella executive throughout the early Resident Evil games?
choices: William Birkin, lead Umbrella scientist and G-Virus creator
Brian Irons, the corrupt Raccoon City police chief
Oswell E. Spencer, the co-founder of Umbrella Corporation
context: Albert Wesker betrayed his S.T.A.R.S. teammates in the original Resident Evil, revealing himself as an Umbrella double agent. He injected himself with a virus that gave him superhuman abilities, including incredible speed and strength. His sunglasses-wearing, trench-coat villain persona became iconic in gaming.

question: What is the name of the relentless pursuer in Resident Evil 3?
choices: Mr. X, the Tyrant deployed during the Raccoon City outbreak
Tyrant T-103, the mass-produced bio-weapon prototype
Hunter Alpha, the genetically engineered reptilian B.O.W.
context: Nemesis is a Tyrant bio-weapon programmed to hunt and kill all remaining S.T.A.R.S. members in Raccoon City. Unlike previous enemies, Nemesis can use weapons, run, and follow the player between areas. His terrifying 'STAAARS!' growl became one of gaming's most recognizable villain lines.

question: In Resident Evil 4, what country does Leon travel to?
choices: Romania, a rural village near the Carpathians
Russia, in a remote Siberian province
Eastern Europe, near an unspecified border region
context: Leon travels to rural Spain to rescue the President's daughter from the Los Illuminados cult. The game's shift from zombie horror to action-oriented gameplay against intelligent, weapon-wielding Ganados revolutionized third-person shooters. The over-the-shoulder camera angle it popularized was adopted by countless games afterward.

question: What are the parasite-controlled villagers in RE4 called?
context: Ganados (Spanish for 'cattle') are villagers infected with Las Plagas parasites that control their minds while maintaining their intelligence and ability to use tools and weapons. Unlike mindless zombies, they can set ambushes, use chainsaws, and coordinate attacks. The Las Plagas represented a major evolution in RE enemy design.

question: Who is Leon Kennedy sent to rescue in Resident Evil 4?
choices: Ada Wong, a mysterious double agent working for Umbrella
Claire Redfield, the sister of STARS member Chris Redfield
Sherry Birkin, a government official's kidnapped child
context: Ashley Graham is the US President's daughter, kidnapped by the Los Illuminados cult to be implanted with a Las Plagas parasite and returned as a sleeper agent. Protecting Ashley while fighting enemies became a central gameplay mechanic. Her frequent cries of 'LEON, HELP!' became a beloved meme.

question: Who is the main villain of Resident Evil Village (RE8)?
choices: Lady Dimitrescu, the towering vampire countess of the castle
Karl Heisenberg, the metal-controlling factory lord
Ethan Winters, the protagonist infected by the Megamycete
context: Mother Miranda is an ancient being who mastered the Megamycete (a massive mold colony) and created the four lords of the village in her experiments to resurrect her dead daughter. Lady Dimitrescu, Heisenberg, Moreau, and Beneviento are all her pawns. Miranda's centuries of manipulation make her one of RE's most dangerous villains.

question: What is the name of the tall vampire woman in RE8 Village?
choices: Lady Carmilla, the blood-drinking countess of Castle Lupu
Miranda Dimitrescu, daughter of the village's founder
Countess Moroaică, the ancient ruler of the eastern territories
context: Lady Alcina Dimitrescu stands 9 feet 6 inches tall and rules her castle section with her three vampire daughters. Her towering presence and elegant menace made her an instant fan sensation before the game even launched. Her mutations are caused by the Cadou parasite, not traditional vampirism.

question: What corporation is responsible for most biological outbreaks in Resident Evil?
choices: Tricell Pharmaceuticals, a rival bioweapons firm
The Connections, an underground bioweapons ring
Umbrella's shadow division, the Blue Umbrella
context: The Umbrella Corporation was a pharmaceutical company that secretly developed biological weapons, ultimately causing the Raccoon City disaster and numerous other outbreaks. Despite being 'officially' dissolved, its research and former employees continue to cause problems throughout the series. Their red-and-white logo is one of gaming's most recognized corporate symbols.

question: What is William Birkin's mutation in Resident Evil 2?
choices: Tyrant mutation from experimental T-Virus exposure
Nemesis parasite infection altering his physiology
Las Plagas-style mutation triggered by Umbrella scientists
context: William Birkin injected himself with his own G-Virus to survive being shot by Umbrella's USS team, triggering a series of grotesque mutations that make him increasingly monstrous. He transforms through five stages, each more horrifying than the last. His G-Virus is more powerful than the T-Virus but harder to control.

question: What does S.T.A.R.S. stand for in Resident Evil?
context: Special Tactics And Rescue Service is an elite law enforcement unit of the Raccoon City Police Department. Both S.T.A.R.S. Alpha and Bravo teams are sent to investigate bizarre murders in the Arklay Mountains, leading them to the Spencer Mansion. Most members are killed, with only Chris, Jill, Barry, Brad, and Rebecca surviving.

question: What is the name of the fungal entity in RE7 that Eveline controls?
choices: The Mycete, a neural fungal network binding the infected
The Spore, an airborne pathogen released by Eveline
The Root, a subterranean fungal organism in the Baker estate
context: The Mold (Mutamycete) is a fungal superorganism that can infect and control living beings, giving them regenerative abilities and linking their minds. Eveline, a bioweapon made from the Mold, controls the infected Baker family through it. The Mold connects to RE Village through the Megamycete, the Mold's origin.

question: Who is Chris Redfield's partner in the original Resident Evil?
choices: Claire Redfield, Chris's younger sister and BSAA ally
Rebecca Chambers, the medic of S.T.A.R.S. Bravo team
Ada Wong, a spy embedded within the Raccoon City outbreak
context: Jill Valentine is a member of S.T.A.R.S. Alpha team and one of the series' two original protagonists. She and Chris can be chosen as separate playable characters in RE1, each with different gameplay experiences. Jill's lockpicking skills and access to Barry Burton's help make her campaign slightly easier.

question: What is the first shout the Dragonborn learns in Skyrim?
choices: Fire Breath — a shout learned from dragons later in the game
Whirlwind Sprint — a shout used for rapid forward movement
Become Ethereal — a shout granting brief invulnerability
context: Unrelenting Force (Fus Ro Dah) is learned at Bleak Falls Barrow and the Greybeards' High Hrothgar. The first word 'Fus' (Force) is found on a Word Wall in the barrow. 'Fus Ro Dah' became a massive internet meme, with videos of people and objects being blasted away set to the shout's sound effect. Also referred to as: Fus Ro Dah.

question: What is the name of the main antagonist dragon in Skyrim?
choices: Paarthurnax — the elder dragon who leads the Greybeards
Miraak — the first Dragonborn, antagonist of the Dragonborn DLC
Odahviing — a dragon who serves Alduin but can be captured
context: Alduin is the World-Eater, the firstborn of the dragon god Akatosh, prophesied to devour the world and end the current era (kalpa). He was cast forward in time by ancient Nords using an Elder Scroll, emerging in Skyrim's present day. Despite being a world-ending threat, many players find him surprisingly easy compared to random high-level dragons.

question: What is the name of the warrior faction in Skyrim associated with werewolves?
choices: The Fighters Guild — the warrior faction from earlier Elder Scrolls games
The Dawnguard — a faction formed to hunt vampires in the Dawnguard DLC
The Silver Hand — a group of werewolf hunters who oppose the Companions
context: The Companions are an ancient order of warriors based in Whiterun's mead hall Jorrvaskr, built around the overturned ship of the legendary Ysgramor. Their inner circle, the Circle, carries the 'beast blood' of Hircine the Daedric Prince, allowing them to transform into werewolves. The questline forces you to confront whether this gift is a blessing or a curse.

question: Who is the true leader of the Thieves Guild (as a traitor) in Skyrim?
choices: Brynjolf — the charismatic recruiter who first brings you into the Guild
Vex — a senior Guild member and expert lockpick specialist
Maven Black-Briar — the powerful noble with deep ties to the Guild
context: Mercer Frey was the Thieves Guild Guildmaster who secretly stole the Skeleton Key of Nocturnal, breaking the guild's oath and causing their string of bad luck. He'd been embezzling from the Guild for years and murdered the previous Guildmaster. His betrayal and the quest to restore Nocturnal's artifact is one of Skyrim's best storylines.

question: What is the name of the hidden assassin faction in Skyrim?
choices: Brotherhood of Shadows — a fictional assassin guild with no Elder Scrolls connection
Morag Tong — the rival assassin guild legal in Morrowind but absent from Skyrim
The Penitus Oculatus — the Emperor's personal guard who oppose the Brotherhood
context: The Dark Brotherhood is a family of assassins who worship Sithis and the Night Mother, accepting contracts through a ritual called the Black Sacrament. Their greeting 'What is the music of life? Silence, my brother' is one of gaming's most memorable faction passwords. Their Skyrim questline involves an assassination plot against the Emperor himself.

question: Which Daedric Prince is associated with madness and cheese in Elder Scrolls?
choices: Mehrunes Dagon, Prince of Destruction and Revolution
Molag Bal, Prince of Domination and Enslavement
Clavicus Vile, Prince of Wishes and Bargains
context: Sheogorath, the Daedric Prince of Madness, is one of the most beloved characters in the Elder Scrolls series. His obsession with cheese (especially cheese wheels) has become a fan-favorite running joke. In Skyrim, his quest 'The Mind of Madness' takes place inside the mind of the dead Emperor Pelagius III.

question: What is the name of the wise old dragon who lives on the Throat of the World?
choices: Arngeir, the speaking leader of the Greybeards
Einarth, a senior Greybeard master of the Thu'um
Borri, a silent elder who trains the Dragonborn
context: Paarthurnax is the ancient dragon who leads the Greybeards from atop the Throat of the World, where he has spent millennia meditating to overcome his violent dragon nature. He was once Alduin's lieutenant before betraying him to teach mortals the Thu'um. The Blades' demand that you kill him creates one of Skyrim's most debated moral choices.

question: What is the name of the civil war leader of the Stormcloaks in Skyrim?
choices: General Tullius, commander of the Imperial Legion in Skyrim
Galmar Stone-Fist, the Stormcloak housecarl and military commander
Brunwulf Free-Winter, a Nord veteran critical of Stormcloak views
context: Ulfric Stormcloak is the Jarl of Windhelm who killed High King Torygg with the Thu'um and ignited the Skyrim Civil War. His cause (Nordic independence and Talos worship) has legitimate grievances, but his methods and the racism in Windhelm complicate his heroic image. Players must choose between the Stormcloaks and the Imperial Legion.

question: What are the words of power in Skyrim called collectively?
choices: Dovahzul, the ancient written language of the dragons
Shouting of the Voice, the Way of the Greybeards
Krii Lun Aus, the art of spoken dragon power
context: The Thu'um (Dragon Shout) is the ancient art of using the dragon language to project power through voice. Each shout consists of three words of power, with each word increasing its potency. The Dragonborn can learn shouts instantly by absorbing dragon souls, while the Greybeards spent decades mastering even basic shouts.

question: What is the title given to one who can absorb dragon souls in Skyrim?
choices: Greybeard, a master of the Thu'um who trains at High Hrothgar
Dovahsebrom, one prophesied to challenge Alduin at the end of time
Tongues, a warrior who has mastered the Way of the Voice
context: Dovahkiin (Dragonborn) literally means 'Dragonborn' in the dragon language: 'Dovah' (dragon) + 'kiin' (born). The Dragonborn has the body of a mortal but the soul of a dragon, allowing them to absorb the knowledge and power of slain dragons. The prophecy of the Last Dragonborn is inscribed on Alduin's Wall.

question: Which city in Skyrim is the capital of the Stormcloak rebellion?
choices: Whiterun, the central trade hub of Skyrim
Solitude, the Imperial capital of Skyrim
Riften, home of the Thieves Guild
context: Windhelm is the oldest city in Skyrim, founded by Ysgramor himself, and serves as Ulfric Stormcloak's seat of power. The city's Grey Quarter, where Dunmer refugees are confined to squalid conditions, reveals uncomfortable truths about the Stormcloak ideology. Its Palace of the Kings is one of the most impressive buildings in the game.

question: Who is the Daedric Prince of Destruction associated with the planes of Oblivion?
choices: Molag Bal, the Daedric Prince of Domination and Enslavement
Boethiah, the Daedric Prince of Deceit and Betrayal
Hermaeus Mora, the Daedric Prince of Forbidden Knowledge
context: Mehrunes Dagon is the Daedric Prince of Destruction, Change, and Ambition who was the main antagonist of The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion. He attempted a full-scale invasion of Tamriel through Oblivion Gates. His artifact, Mehrunes' Razor, is a dagger with a small chance to instantly kill any enemy in Skyrim.

question: What is the name of the vampire faction in the Dawnguard DLC?
choices: The Sanguine Court, servants of the Daedric Prince of Revelry
The Crimson Shroud, an ancient vampire coven from Morrowind
Children of the Night, worshippers of Molag Bal
context: The Volkihar Clan is led by Lord Harkon, an ancient vampire who seeks to fulfill a prophecy to blot out the sun permanently using Auriel's Bow. Players can choose to join the vampires or the Dawnguard vampire hunters. The Volkihar castle on a frozen island is one of Skyrim's most atmospheric locations.

question: What is the name of the ancient axe of the Companions in Skyrim?
choices: Dragonbane, a blade forged specifically to slay dragons
Skyforge Steel Axe, the masterwork weapon of the Companions
Harbinger's Cleave, the ceremonial axe of the Companions' leader
context: Wuuthrad was the legendary battleaxe wielded by Ysgramor during the Return, when he led 500 Companions to reclaim Skyrim from the elves. The axe was shattered and its fragments scattered over the centuries. Reassembling Wuuthrad is the key to entering Ysgramor's Tomb during the Companions questline.

question: In Skyrim, what skill tree governs the use of one-handed weapons?
choices: Melee Combat, covering all close-quarters weapon skills
Warrior, the primary skill tree for physical fighters
context: The One-Handed skill tree includes swords, war axes, maces, and daggers. Its perks branch into specialized paths for different weapon types, with Armsman increasing base damage and specialized perks adding bleeding, decapitation, and paralyzing effects. Most combat-focused characters invest heavily in this tree.

question: What is the name of the Daedric Prince who rules the Shivering Isles?
choices: Hermaeus Mora, Prince of Forbidden Knowledge
Sanguine, Prince of Debauchery and Revelry
context: Sheogorath rules the Shivering Isles, a realm of madness split between Mania (creative insanity) and Dementia (dark insanity). The Oblivion expansion of the same name is widely considered one of the best Elder Scrolls DLCs ever made. In it, the player actually becomes Sheogorath, which is why Skyrim's Sheogorath makes references to the events of Oblivion.

question: Who is the main villain of Final Fantasy VII?
choices: President Shinra, the ruthless corporate tyrant
Rufus Shinra, who takes control of the Shinra Corporation
Jenova, the alien entity whose cells shaped Sephiroth
context: Sephiroth is a former SOLDIER hero who went insane upon discovering he was created from cells of the alien entity Jenova. His one-winged angel form and iconic theme song 'One-Winged Angel' made him gaming's most recognizable villain. His murder of Aerith is one of the most shocking moments in video game history.

question: What is the name of Cloud's giant sword in FF7?
choices: Masamune, the impossibly long blade wielded by Sephiroth
Ultima Weapon, a powerful late-game sword tied to Cloud's HP
Hardedge, an early Midgar weapon favored for its raw attack power
context: The Buster Sword is a massive, iconic blade originally wielded by Cloud's friend and mentor Zack Fair. Cloud inherited it after Zack's death, and it serves as a symbol of their bond and Cloud's false memories. The sword's impractical size has become a beloved trope of Japanese RPG character design.

question: What is the name of Cloud's childhood friend in Final Fantasy VII?
choices: Aerith Gainsborough, the last surviving Cetra who joins AVALANCHE
Yuffie Kisaragi, a Wutai ninja who joins the party mid-journey
Jessie Rasberry, Cloud's AVALANCHE comrade based in Midgar
context: Tifa Lockhart grew up in Nibelheim with Cloud and runs the 7th Heaven bar that serves as AVALANCHE's secret headquarters. She's one of the few people who knows the truth about Cloud's fabricated memories. Her martial arts fighting style makes her unique among FF7's cast of sword and gun wielders.

question: Who is Yuna's main guardian in Final Fantasy X?
choices: Auron, the legendary guardian who guided both Braska and Yuna
Wakka, the devout Yevonite captain of the Besaid Aurochs
Kimahri Ronso, the stoic guardian who raised Yuna since childhood
context: Tidus is a star Blitzball player from the dream city of Zanarkand who is thrown 1,000 years into the future and becomes Yuna's guardian on her pilgrimage. His cheerful personality gradually reveals deeper layers as the truth about Spira's cycle of destruction unfolds. The laugh scene between him and Yuna is intentionally awkward, not bad acting.

question: What is the recurring fire summon in Final Fantasy?
choices: Shiva, the recurring ice summon maiden
Leviathan, the recurring water serpent summon
Ramuh, the recurring lightning elder summon
context: Ifrit has appeared in nearly every mainline Final Fantasy game since FFIII, typically as a horned, muscular fire demon. He's usually one of the earliest summons obtained and serves as a reliable source of fire damage. His design has evolved dramatically across the series, from a simple demon to an elaborate beast.

question: What does a Moogle say in every Final Fantasy appearance?
choices: Mogri, a greeting used only in Final Fantasy IX
Kupopo, the formal version used by elder Moogles
Mog, their self-referential name used as an exclamation
context: Moogles say 'Kupo!' as their signature expression, and it's become one of Final Fantasy's most recognizable catchphrases. These small, fluffy creatures with bat wings and a pom-pom antenna have served as save points, mail carriers, shopkeepers, and even party members across the series. They first appeared in Final Fantasy III.

question: Who is the main villain of Final Fantasy VI?
choices: Emperor Gestahl, the ruthless ruler of the Gestahlian Empire
Ultros, the comic-relief octopus recurring boss
Atma Weapon, the ancient magical guardian on the Floating Continent
context: Kefka Palazzo is a nihilistic court jester who actually succeeds in destroying the world and becoming a god, making him unique among FF villains. His maniacal laugh and clown-like appearance mask genuine menace. He's often cited as one of gaming's greatest villains because he's one of the few who actually wins.

question: What does FF6's World of Ruin refer to?
answer: The second half of the game after Kefka destroys the world
choices: A collapsed dimensional rift where the Espers were imprisoned
The second half of the game after Kefka destroys the world
The shattered remains of the ancient Esper homeland
The devastated floating continent after it crashes into the sea
context: Halfway through Final Fantasy VI, Kefka actually destroys the world by rearranging the Warring Triad statues, creating the World of Ruin. The second half of the game takes place a year later in this devastated landscape. The player must reassemble their party one by one, with most character reunions being optional.

question: What is the large yellow bird creature used for transportation in Final Fantasy?
choices: Moogle, the small winged creature that assists players
Cactuar, the spiny desert creature known for its 1000 Needles attack
Tonberry, the slow-moving hooded creature wielding a knife and lantern
context: Chocobos have appeared in every mainline Final Fantasy since FFII and are the series' most beloved mascot alongside Moogles. They come in various colors with different abilities (gold chocobos can fly and cross water). Their iconic 'Chocobo Theme' music is one of gaming's most recognizable tunes.

question: What is the name of the recurring Final Fantasy character who is always an engineer or airship pilot?
choices: Biggs, a recurring soldier who appears in multiple games
Wedge, Biggs' comedic companion throughout the series
Edgar, the mechanist king of Figaro Castle
context: A character named Cid appears in every mainline Final Fantasy from FFII onward, always connected to technology, engineering, or airships in some way. Each Cid is a different character, from gruff mechanics to refined scientists to playable party members. The tradition has become one of the series' most beloved Easter eggs.

question: In FF10, what is the name of the water sport played in Spira?
choices: Sphere Break, the coin-based minigame from FFX-2
Triton Cup, the underwater tournament held in Bevelle
Shoopuf Racing, a popular sport along the Moonflow
context: Blitzball is an underwater sport played inside a massive sphere of water, where players can hold their breath for extended periods. It's a fully playable minigame in FFX with its own recruitment system, stats, and tournaments. Tidus was a star player for the Zanarkand Abes before being transported to Spira.

question: What is Sephiroth's most powerful attack?
choices: Meteor, a planet-destroying spell he uses to threaten the world
Heartless Angel, which reduces the target's HP to near zero
Shadow Flare, a powerful dark-energy attack used in later phases
context: Supernova is an absurdly cinematic attack where Sephiroth summons a meteor that destroys the entire solar system (in the international version), complete with a two-minute unskippable animation. Despite the apocalyptic visuals, it deals percentage-based damage and can't actually kill your party. The Japanese version has a shorter, different animation.

question: What was the original Final Fantasy game released on?
choices: SNES / Super Famicom, home to Final Fantasy IV through VI
MSX2, a popular Japanese home computer of the late 1980s
PC-88, NEC's dominant Japanese personal computer platform
context: The original Final Fantasy (1987) was released on the Nintendo Entertainment System (Famicom in Japan). Creator Hironobu Sakaguchi named it 'Final Fantasy' because he planned to quit the industry if it failed. It became a massive hit and spawned one of gaming's most successful franchises.

question: In Final Fantasy IX, who is the main protagonist?
choices: Vivi Ornitier, the young black mage searching for his identity
Garnet til Alexandros, the princess who flees her own kingdom
Adelbert Steiner, the loyal captain of the Alexandria Knights
context: Zidane Tribal is a charming, monkey-tailed thief and member of the Tantalus Theater Troupe. FFIX was designed as a love letter to the classic, more fantastical style of earlier Final Fantasy games. Zidane's upbeat, flirtatious personality was a deliberate contrast to the brooding Cloud and Squall of FFVII and FFVIII.

question: What is Terra's unique ability in Final Fantasy VI?
answer: She can use magic naturally without espers
choices: She can dual-wield any weapon without penalties
She can use magic naturally without espers
She has no MP limit and regenerates mana passively
She can transform into an Esper at will in battle
context: Terra Branford is half-human, half-Esper, making her the only person in the world who can use magic naturally without magicite. She can also transform into her Esper form, dramatically boosting her power. Her identity crisis about being caught between two worlds is the emotional core of FFVI's first half.

question: What is the name of the recurring blue dragon deity summon in Final Fantasy?
context: Bahamut is the King of Dragons and typically the most powerful non-secret summon in each Final Fantasy game, unleashing devastating Megaflare attacks. He appears in almost every FF title and has had many forms (Bahamut ZERO, Neo Bahamut, etc.). His name comes from the mythological Bahamut of Arabian mythology. Note: Bahamut is depicted as a dragon, not specifically blue — the question's description may be inaccurate.

question: What opera is performed in Final Fantasy VI?
choices: Aria di Mezzo Carattere, the soprano showcase piece
context: The 'Maria and Draco' opera scene is one of the most beloved sequences in RPG history, where Celes must perform as the lead soprano to lure the opera-loving airship pilot Setzer. 'Aria di Mezzo Carattere' is the name of the famous song performed within the opera, not the opera itself. The scene was fully voiced and orchestrated in the Pixel Remaster.

question: What is the name of the mega-city built on metal plates in FF7?
context: Midgar is a dystopian megacity built on massive plates that literally block sunlight from the slums below, creating a stark class divide. The Shinra Electric Power Company controls the city and drains the planet's Lifestream for energy. Midgar's oppressive atmosphere and environmental themes were groundbreaking for 1997.

question: Who are the original 8 characters in Super Smash Bros. 64?
answer: Mario, Donkey Kong, Link, Samus, Yoshi, Kirby, Fox, Pikachu
choices: Mario, Luigi, Link, Samus, Yoshi, Kirby, Fox, Pikachu
Mario, Donkey Kong, Link, Samus, Yoshi, Kirby, Fox, Pikachu
Mario, Donkey Kong, Link, Zelda, Yoshi, Kirby, Fox, Pikachu
Mario, Donkey Kong, Link, Samus, Yoshi, Kirby, Falco, Pikachu
context: The original eight were Nintendo's most iconic characters: Mario, Donkey Kong, Link, Samus, Yoshi, Kirby, Fox, and Pikachu. Luigi was notably absent from the starting roster (he was an unlockable). Creator Masahiro Sakurai developed the original prototype as 'Dragon King: The Fighting Game' with generic characters before adding Nintendo stars.

question: What is the technique in Melee that lets characters slide on the ground after an air dodge?
context: Wavedashing involves jumping and immediately air-dodging diagonally into the ground, causing the character to slide while retaining all ground options. It was an unintended mechanic that became central to high-level Melee play. Nintendo never patched it out and it's now considered a defining feature of Melee's competitive scene.

question: What item in Smash Bros. activates a character's Final Smash?
context: The Smash Ball is a floating, glowing orb that must be hit multiple times to break, granting the character who breaks it access to their unique Final Smash super move. It was introduced in Super Smash Bros. Brawl. Ultimate also added the Final Smash Meter as an alternative way to charge Final Smashes.

question: What is Captain Falcon's most famous move?
context: The Falcon Punch is an enormously powerful, dramatically slow fire-infused punch that has become one of gaming's most iconic attacks. It's impractical in competitive play due to its long wind-up, but landing it is immensely satisfying. Captain Falcon's 'FALCON PUNCH!' scream has been memed endlessly since 1999.

question: Which Smash Bros. game introduced the Subspace Emissary story mode?
choices: Super Smash Bros. for Wii U
context: Super Smash Bros. Brawl (2008) featured the Subspace Emissary, a full side-scrolling adventure mode with cutscenes showing Nintendo characters teaming up. It was directed by Masahiro Sakurai, who poured enormous effort into the cinematic crossover story. The leaked cutscenes online before release led Sakurai to skip story modes in later games.

question: What game is Meta Knight from?
context: Meta Knight is from the Kirby series, where he serves as a mysterious, honorable rival to Kirby. He always offers Kirby a sword before fighting, showing his code of fair combat. In Super Smash Bros. Brawl, he was infamously the best character in the game and was sometimes banned from tournaments.

question: What character is widely considered the best in Melee's competitive tier list?
choices: Marth, a fast swordfighter with strong edge game
Falco, a strong Fox clone with better combos
Jigglypuff, known for deadly rest and edgeguarding
context: Fox McCloud's incredible speed, combo potential, and devastating 'shine' (Reflector) make him Melee's consensus top-tier character. His technical ceiling is so high that even top players can't execute everything perfectly. The 'Fox ditto' (Fox vs. Fox) at high level is considered Melee's purest test of skill.

question: What was the final DLC fighter added to Super Smash Bros. Ultimate?
choices: Master Chief, the iconic Halo protagonist from Xbox
Waluigi, the fan-favorite meme candidate from Mario
Crash Bandicoot, the beloved 90s PlayStation mascot
context: Sora from Kingdom Hearts was the final DLC fighter, revealed at a special presentation in October 2021. His inclusion was the result of years of negotiations between Nintendo, Disney, and Square Enix. Sakurai revealed that Sora was the most-requested character in the Smash Fighter Ballot.

question: What technique in Melee involves canceling the landing lag of aerial attacks?
choices: Shine spiking, which uses Fox's reflector to meteor smash
Crouch canceling, which reduces knockback by crouching on landing
Teching, which lets you recover from hitting a wall or floor
context: L-canceling (also called Z-canceling in Smash 64) halves the landing lag of aerial attacks when you press L, R, or Z just before landing. It's one of the first advanced techniques competitive players learn. Nintendo removed it in later Smash games, reducing the technical barrier but also the skill expression.

question: How many total fighters are in Super Smash Bros. Ultimate including all DLC?
choices: 76, which was the roster size at the game's initial launch
82, the number of base roster fighters before any DLC
91, counting each Echo Fighter as a fully separate character
context: Super Smash Bros. Ultimate has 89 fighters (82 base roster fighters plus 12 DLC fighters, with Pyra and Mythra sharing one slot). Its 'Everyone is Here!' tagline meant every single previous Smash character returned. Sakurai has said this roster size is unlikely to be matched in any future installment.

question: What move does Ness use that reflects projectiles and absorbs PSI moves?
choices: PK Shield, which creates a temporary barrier against all attacks
PSI Reflect, which sends projectiles back at double speed
PK Counter, which absorbs hits and retaliates with psychic energy
context: PSI Magnet creates an energy field around Ness that absorbs energy-based projectiles and converts them into healing. It's essential for Ness's survival against projectile-heavy characters. The move comes from EarthBound, where PSI Magnet absorbs enemy psychic attacks. Note that PSI Magnet does not reflect projectiles — it absorbs them for health, and only works on energy-based attacks.

question: Which Smash Bros. game had the Adventure Mode called 'The Subspace Emissary'?
choices: Super Smash Bros. for Wii U/3DS
context: Brawl's Subspace Emissary was a massive side-scrolling adventure featuring Nintendo characters facing an original villain, Tabuu. The mode featured gorgeous CG cutscenes showing characters from different franchises meeting for the first time. Its ambitious scope hasn't been replicated in later Smash games.

question: What is Bowser's species?
context: Bowser is a Koopa, specifically the King of the Koopas, a turtle-like species in the Mario universe. His Japanese name 'Kuppa' comes from the Korean word for a type of rice soup. Despite being Mario's arch-nemesis, he's also a loving father to Bowser Jr. and occasionally teams up with Mario against greater threats.

question: What power-up lets Mario shoot fireballs?
context: The Fire Flower transforms Mario into Fire Mario (white and red outfit), granting the ability to throw bouncing fireballs. It first appeared in Super Mario Bros. (1985) and has been in nearly every Mario game since. In multiplayer games, Luigi's fireballs are green while Mario's are red.

question: Who holds the Triforce of Wisdom in the Legend of Zelda?
context: Princess Zelda holds the Triforce of Wisdom, which grants her prophetic dreams, magical abilities, and deep insight. Despite the series being named after her, she's been playable in relatively few games. Each game features a different incarnation of Zelda, all descended from the goddess Hylia.

question: What is the famous reveal about Samus Aran at the end of the original Metroid?
answer: She is a woman, revealed based on completion time
choices: She is a Chozo-human hybrid, not fully human
She is a woman, revealed based on completion time
She is an ancient AI created by the Galactic Federation
She is a clone of the original Samus who died on K-2L
context: Completing the original Metroid quickly enough reveals that the armored bounty hunter Samus is a woman, one of gaming's most groundbreaking twists in 1986. The faster you beat the game, the more of her civilian appearance you see. This reveal challenged assumptions about who video game heroes could be.

question: What is the name of Donkey Kong's main villain?
choices: Bowser, the Koopa King from the Mario series
Klubba, the Kremling toll bridge enforcer
Kaptain Skurvy, the pirate rival of DK
context: King K. Rool is a crocodile king who repeatedly steals Donkey Kong's banana hoard and kidnaps Kong family members. He's appeared as a pirate (Kaptain K. Rool), a mad scientist (Baron K. Roolenstein), and a boxer (King Krusha K. Rool). His inclusion in Smash Bros. Ultimate was one of the most celebrated character reveals.

question: What is the name of Kirby's home world?
choices: Dream Land, the peaceful kingdom ruled by King Dedede
Halcandra, the ancient realm of the Four Mage-Sisters
Shiver Star, the ice-covered planet from Kirby 64
context: Planet Popstar is a star-shaped planet in the Kirby universe, with Dream Land being its most famous kingdom. Kirby protects it from threats ranging from the evil King Dedede to cosmic horrors like Dark Matter. Despite its cute appearance, the Kirby series has some of gaming's most terrifyingly powerful final bosses.

question: What is Captain Falcon's racing vehicle called?
choices: Falcon Flyer, the aerial transport from Smash Bros.
Mach Rider, the high-speed pursuit vehicle
White Cat, the rival machine piloted by Blood Falcon
context: The Blue Falcon is Captain Falcon's signature F-Zero racing machine, capable of speeds exceeding 1,500 km/h. F-Zero (1990) was one of the SNES launch titles and showcased Mode 7 graphics. Despite Captain Falcon's massive popularity from Smash Bros., Nintendo hasn't released a new F-Zero game since 2004.

question: What is the first dungeon in Ocarina of Time called?
choices: Forest Temple, the haunted dungeon in the Sacred Forest Meadow
Dodongo's Cavern, the explosive-filled lizard lair
Kokiri Training Grounds, the hidden shrine of the forest children
context: Inside the Deku Tree is the first dungeon, where young Link must clear a curse placed by Ganondorf. The Great Deku Tree serves as the guardian of Kokiri Forest and Link's initial mentor. Despite Link's success, the Deku Tree dies from the curse, giving the game an unexpectedly somber early tone.

question: What does Ganondorf's piece of the Triforce represent?
choices: Courage, the virtue held by the hero Link
Wisdom, the sacred relic entrusted to Princess Zelda
Strength, the force that fuels Ganondorf's dark armies
context: Ganondorf holds the Triforce of Power, which grants him near-invulnerability and immense dark magic. His relentless pursuit of the complete Triforce drives the conflict in most Zelda games. He's the recurring incarnation of the demon king Demise's hatred, cursed to eternally battle the hero and the goddess.

question: What is the main lord character in Fire Emblem: Path of Radiance?
choices: Roy, the young lord of Pherae from Elibe
Marth, the Hero-King of Altea from Archanea
Ephraim, the prince of Renais from Magvel
context: Ike is the son of the mercenary Greil and leads the Greil Mercenaries into a continent-spanning war. His lack of noble birth made him unique among Fire Emblem lords and contributed to his popularity. His famous Smash Bros. line 'I fight for my friends' perfectly captures his straightforward character.

question: What is the final boss of The Legend of Zelda: Majora's Mask?
choices: Skull Kid, the troubled youth possessed by the mask
Ganondorf, the Gerudo King of Evil from Hyrule
The Moon's spirit, drawn down by Majora's power
context: Majora's Mask goes through three forms: Majora's Mask (the possessed mask), Majora's Incarnation (a bizarre dancing creature), and Majora's Wrath (a terrifying whip-armed humanoid). The mask itself is an ancient artifact of terrible power that possessed Skull Kid. The fight takes place inside the Moon, which has been threatening to crash into Termina for three days.

question: What does Meta Knight's mask conceal?
answer: A face almost identical to Kirby's
choices: A scarred, battle-worn face he keeps hidden in shame
A face almost identical to Kirby's
A mechanical visage installed after a near-fatal wound
An empty void, as he is a being of pure darkness
context: Beneath Meta Knight's mask is a face nearly identical to Kirby's, just blue instead of pink. He hides it because he considers showing his face embarrassing, immediately fleeing when his mask breaks. This connection has led to decades of fan speculation about whether he and Kirby are the same species.

question: What are the four Divine Beasts in Breath of the Wild named after?
choices: The four elemental spirits of Hyrule's sacred temples
The four races that built the Divine Beasts for Ganon
Four ancient sages who sealed Calamity Ganon away
context: Vah Ruta (Ruto), Vah Medoh (Medli), Vah Rudania (Darunia), and Vah Naboris (Nabooru) are each named after sages from previous Zelda games, specifically Ocarina of Time and Wind Waker. Each was piloted by a Champion who fell to Calamity Ganon 100 years ago. Freeing the Divine Beasts provides powerful assistance in the final battle.

question: What is the name of the Metroid homeworld where Metroids originate?
choices: Planet Zebes, the Chozo stronghold later overtaken by Space Pirates
Aether, the dark-energy-rich planet from Metroid Prime 2
Planet Phaaze, the living Phazon world from Metroid Prime 3
context: SR388 is a hostile planet where the Chozo created Metroids to combat the parasitic X organisms. The entire planet was explored in Metroid II: Return of Samus and its remake Metroid: Samus Returns. Samus's mission to exterminate all Metroids on SR388 sets up the events of Super Metroid and Metroid Fusion.

question: What is the premise of the game Grounded?
answer: Teenagers are shrunk to insect-size in a suburban backyard
choices: Players shrink themselves to explore a miniature alien world
Teenagers are shrunk to insect-size in a suburban backyard
Scientists are accidentally miniaturized inside a research laboratory
Children survive after being shrunk and abandoned in a city park
context: Grounded by Obsidian Entertainment shrinks four teenagers to the size of ants in a suburban backyard, turning ordinary grass into a jungle and garden bugs into terrifying monsters. The game draws heavy inspiration from the 1989 film 'Honey, I Shrunk the Kids.' Its survival mechanics are built around the unique perspective of being tiny.

question: What are the names of the four playable characters in Grounded?
context: Max, Pete, Willow, and Hoops each have distinct personalities: Max is the leader type, Pete is the nerdy one, Willow is the rebellious one, and Hoops is the athletic one. They wake up shrunk in the backyard with no memory of how it happened. Each character has the same gameplay abilities.

question: What is the name of the robot that gives quests to players in Grounded?
context: BURG.L is a helpful but glitchy robot who provides quests, recipes, and information about the backyard. He is missing several of his science chips, which players must recover from dangerous locations around the yard. His cheerful and upbeat personality makes him the game's most endearing NPC.

question: What device do players use to analyze items and unlock recipes in Grounded?
choices: A handheld scanner called the MiniScope
context: The SCA.B (Super Chip Attached to Back) is a wrist-mounted device that serves as the player's HUD, map, and item analyzer. Analyzing new materials unlocks crafting recipes. It's a brilliantly practical plot device that explains how teenagers can build complex equipment from insect parts and plant matter.

question: What corporation is responsible for the shrinking experiment in Grounded?
context: Ominent Diversified Sciences is the shadowy corporation behind the miniaturization technology that shrunk the kids. Their experiments in the backyard lab go far beyond simple shrinking, with evidence of more sinister research uncovered through scattered logs and documents found throughout the yard.

question: Who is the scientist responsible for the shrinking experiment in Grounded?
context: Dr. Wendell Tully created the shrinking technology and built the backyard research lab, but something went wrong during his experiments. Players gradually piece together his story through audio logs and lab discoveries. His motivations become more complex as the story unfolds.

question: What is the most feared large spider in Grounded?
context: Wolf Spiders are nocturnal hunters that roam the backyard at night, and encountering one unprepared is often fatal for new players. They're fast, aggressive, and deal massive damage. The game actually has an arachnophobia mode that progressively reduces spiders to floating blobs to accommodate players with spider fears.

question: What material do players collect from grass blades in Grounded?
choices: Hay Bales and Dried Reed Stalks
context: Grass Planks (chopped from full grass blades) and Grass Fiber (from loose grass) are the game's most basic building materials. They're used for everything from walls and floors to basic tools. Carrying planks is physically modeled, with the tiny character hauling enormous (relative to them) pieces of grass.

question: What large insect in Grounded sprays a burning chemical as an attack?
context: Bombardier Beetles spray a boiling chemical defense from their abdomen, just like their real-world counterparts, which can eject a caustic spray at 100 degrees Celsius. In the game, their acid attack deals heavy damage and can catch unprepared players off guard. Real bombardier beetles create this spray through an internal chemical reaction.

question: What health drinks can be crafted at a smoothie station in Grounded?
context: Smoothies are healing and buff drinks made from various backyard ingredients like berries, flowers, and bug parts at a Smoothie Station. Different recipes provide different effects: healing, stamina recovery, attack boosts, and resistance to specific damage types. The smoothie system encourages experimentation with ingredient combinations.

question: What large red creature in Grounded is found near the oak tree and charges players?
choices: Bombardier Beetle, which spits acid at players
Roly Poly, the armored pill bug that rolls into a ball
Stinkbug, which releases a poisonous gas cloud
context: Ladybugs (called Ladybirds in some regions) are passive creatures that become aggressive when provoked or when players are carrying planks near their territory. Despite being 'cute' in real life, at the player's scale they're intimidating tanks that can kill in a few hits. They're among the first large creatures new players encounter. Also referred to as: Ladybug.

question: What material from spiders is needed to build ziplines in Grounded?
choices: Silk Rope crafted from harvested Orb Weaver thread
Web Fiber Bundle spun from spider den materials
Spider Thread collected from abandoned webs
context: Spider Silk Rope is crafted from spider web, requiring players to venture into spider dens and harvest silk from their webs. This creates a compelling risk-reward dynamic: you need to brave spider territory to build the ziplines that make future travel safer. Ziplines become essential for quickly traversing the vast backyard.

question: What mineral resource in Grounded is mined from white rocks and used for durable tools?
choices: Marble, a smooth white stone found near water sources
Pebblet, the small round rock used in basic crafting
Shard Stone, a crystalline mineral mined from cave walls
context: Quartzite is a tier-2 mineral found in caves and dark areas, used to craft repair tools and upgrade weapons. Mining it requires venturing into dangerous underground areas populated by spiders and other creatures. It's essential for maintaining equipment durability in the mid-game.

question: What are the small round rock resources called that are used in early crafting in Grounded?
choices: Pebbles, the common name for small stones in real life
Stone Chunks, rough fragments broken from larger rocks
Grit Stones, the coarse mineral bits used for grinding
context: Pebblets are tiny pebbles that, at the player's shrunken scale, are the size of boulders. They're used alongside plant fiber and other basic materials to craft early-game tools like axes and hammers. They're one of the first resources new players collect.

question: What is the currency used to buy upgrades from BURG.L in Grounded?
choices: BURG.L Chips, the data cards found in dangerous biomes
Science Tokens, earned by analyzing new insects and plants
Data Fragments, collected from broken SCA.B terminals
context: Raw Science appears as glowing blue orbs scattered throughout the backyard and earned by completing BURG.L's quests. It's spent on purchasing new crafting recipes and upgrades from BURG.L. Finding hidden Raw Science deposits rewards exploration of dangerous areas. Note: BURG.L Chips are separate collectibles that unlock new recipe categories, making them a plausible but incorrect alternative.

question: What is the main antagonist creature type found in the Haze biome in Grounded?
context: The Haze is a foggy area created by a leaking weed killer canister, filled with infected insects including fungal-infested gnats and mites. The toxic atmosphere requires gas masks to survive. It's one of the game's most atmospheric and unsettling biomes.
